topic.name: Contemporary Pop Music
topic.description: The documents discuss various contemporary pop songs and their artists, highlighting their themes, production, and commercial success.

Document 2: 'I Need to Know' is a song recorded by American recording artist Marc Anthony
The song blends the musical styles of several genres, including R & B and Latin music.
Document 3: 'Speechless' is a song written and performed by American singer Lady Gaga
The 1970s rock music-inspired power ballad received mixed reviews from critics.
Document 4: Fame Kills: Starring Kanye West and Lady Gaga was a planned co-headlining concert tour
the pair conceived a production that would unite their different musical audiences.
Document 5: 'Did It Again' is a song by Australian recording artist Kylie Minogue
Critical response to 'Did It Again' was mostly positive.
Document 6: Drowned World Tour was the fifth concert tour by American singer-songwriter Madonna
The show was divided into five segments.
Document 7: 'I Wanna Have Your Babies' is a song by British recording artist Natasha Bedingfield
The song's musical-style and production was inspired by hip hop music.

topic.name: Classic Video Games
topic.description: The documents discuss various classic video games from the late 20th century, highlighting their gameplay, development, and cultural impact.

Document 1: Mega Man 4 is an action-platform game developed by Capcom for the Nintendo Entertainment System.
The game carries on the same action and platforming gameplay as the first three games.
Document 2: The Punisher is a 1993 beat 'em up arcade game developed and released by Capcom.
The game is widely regarded as one of the best titles in the beat 'em up genre.
Document 3: Baldur's Gate II is a role-playing video game developed by BioWare and published by Black Isle Studios.
The game received critical acclaim upon its release and has sold more than two million units.
Document 4: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 is a platform video game developed by Sonic Team and published by Sega.
The game was a critical and commercial success, selling 1.02 million copies in the United States.
Document 5: Ninja Gaiden III is a side-scrolling platforming video game developed and published by Tecmo.
The game is the third installment of the Ninja Gaiden trilogy.
Document 6: Blaster Master is a platform and run and gun video game released by Sunsoft for the Nintendo Entertainment System.
The game features a character named Jason who follows his pet frog Fred down a hole in the earth.
Document 7: Minecraft is a sandbox video game originally created by Swedish programmer Markus 'Notch' Persson.
The creative and building aspects of Minecraft enable players to build constructions out of textured cubes.

topic.name: Political and Legal Issues
topic.description: This cluster addresses various political and legal matters including administrative law, party leadership elections, international naming disputes, independence movements, inquests into deaths in custody, and notable political figures.

Document 1: Threshold issues are legal requirements in Singapore administrative law that must be satisfied by applicants before their claims for judicial review of acts or decisions of public authorities can be dealt with by the High Court.
Applicants must establish they have private standing if they seek to represent personal interests.
Document 2: The 2014 Scottish Labour Party leadership election was an internal party election to choose a new leader and deputy leader of the Scottish Labour Party.

Lamont announced her decision in an interview with the Daily Record on 24 October, saying that she was stepping down effective immediately because the UK Labour Party treated the Scottish party as a 'branch office of London'.
Document 3: The international name for the body of water which is bordered by Japan, North Korea, Russia, and South Korea is disputed.
The Japanese government supports the use of the name 'Sea of Japan', while South Korea supports the name 'East Sea'.
Document 4: Independence Day, observed annually on 15 August is a national holiday in India commemorating the nation 's independence from the British Empire on 15 August 1947.
India attained independence following an Independence Movement noted for largely nonviolent resistance and civil disobedience led by the Indian National Congress.
Document 5: The Ashley Smith inquest was an Ontario coroner 's inquest into the death of Ashley Smith, a teenager who died by self-inflicted strangulation on 19 October 2007 while under suicide watch in custody.
The inquest was frequently interrupted by multiple legal challenges and a change of coroner, before finally being terminated as a mistrial on 30 September 2011.
Document 6: Chrystal Macmillan was a Scottish Liberal politician, barrister, feminist and pacifist, and the first female science graduate from the University of Edinburgh.
She was an activist for women 's right to vote, and for other women 's causes.
Document 7: Nikolai Podgorny was a Soviet statesman during the Cold War.
He served as First Secretary of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of Ukraine from 1957 to 1963 and as Chairman of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet from 1965 to 1977.

topic.name: Cultural Expressions and Morality
topic.description: This cluster explores various forms of cultural expression, including literature, art, and ethical theories, reflecting societal values and individual experiences.

Document 1: Divine command theory proposes that an action's status as morally good is equivalent to whether it is commanded by God.
Followers of both monotheistic and polytheistic religions have often accepted the importance of God's commands in establishing morality.
Document 2: Winnie Winkle was one of the first comic strips about working women.
The main character Winnie was a young woman who had to support her parents and adopted brother, reflecting the changing role of women in society.
Document 3: And Still I Rise focuses on a hopeful determination to rise above difficulty and discouragement.
She speaks for her race and gender in many of the poems, emphasizing the strength and resiliency of her community.
Document 4: Characters of Shakespeare's Plays is among the first English-language studies of Shakespeare's plays to follow the manner of German critic A. W. Schlegel.
The centre of attention is in large part on the characters, described often with a personal slant.
Document 5: The Rocky Mountains, Lander's Peak is based on sketches made during Bierstadt's travels, depicting Native Americans and the landscape.
The painting shows Lander's Peak in the Wyoming Range of the Rocky Mountains.
Document 6: Octave Uzanne is noted for his literary research on the authors of the 18th century.
His research produced a considerable literary output and frequent publications discussing fashion and femininity in the French fin-de-siècle.
Document 7: Dynamic Science Fiction was a pulp magazine which published stories by some well-known authors.
It was launched at the end of the pulp era, reflecting the evolution of the science fiction genre.

topic.name: Atlantic Tropical Cyclones
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclones that occurred during different Atlantic hurricane seasons, detailing their formation, impact, and meteorological history.

Document 1: Tropical Depression One was the wettest tropical cyclone in Cuba since Hurricane Flora of 1963.
the depression became very disorganized as it emerged into the Straits of Florida and degenerated into an open trough.
Document 2: Hurricane Paul was a tropical cyclone that threatened the Baja California peninsula during October 2012.
Paul originated from a trough of low pressure west of the coastline of Mexico.
Document 3: The 1973 Atlantic hurricane season was the first season to use the Saffir – Simpson hurricane scale.
The season produced 24 tropical and subtropical cyclones.
Document 4: Tropical Storm Jerry was a tropical storm that caused severe flooding throughout the southeast United States in August of the 1995 Atlantic hurricane season.
The rainfall it produced, amounting to over 12 inches (300 mm) in several locations across Florida.
Document 5: The 1953 Atlantic hurricane season was the first time an organized list of female names was used to name Atlantic storms.
The season was active with fourteen total storms, six of which developed into hurricanes.
Document 6: Tropical Storm Josephine was an unusual Atlantic tropical storm that moved from west to east across the Gulf of Mexico in October 1996.
The storm also spawned at least 16 tornadoes.
Document 7: Tropical Storm Lidia was a deadly, destructive tropical cyclone that occurred during the 1981 Pacific hurricane season.
It inflicted heavy rain and flooding throughout parts of northwestern Mexico.

topic.name: Motor Racing Events
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various significant motor racing events across different racing series and years.

the team became known as Greaves Motorsport, and they switched to the new Zytek Z11SN, winning the LMP2 category of both the 24 Hours of Le Mans and the Le Mans Series.

Document 3: The 2015 Austrian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 21 June 2015 at the Red Bull Ring in Spielberg, Austria.
Mercedes driver Nico Rosberg entered as the defending race winner.
Document 4: The 2010 São Paulo Indy 300 was the first race of the 2010 IZOD IndyCar Series season.
The race was won by Australian driver Will Power for Team Penske.
Document 5: The 2009 AAA 400 was the twenty-eighth stock car race of the 2009 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.

the film's reputation began to grow during the 1950s.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

Document 1: The 57th Boat Race took place on 31 March 1900.
Cambridge won by twenty lengths in a record-equalling time of 18 minutes 45 seconds.
Document 2: The 110th Boat Race took place on 28 March 1964.
The Oxford crew was the heaviest in Boat Race history.
Document 3: The 140th Boat Race took place on 26 March 1994.
Cambridge won by six-and-a-half lengths.
Document 4: The 96th Boat Race took place on 1 April 1950.
Cambridge won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 20 minutes 15 seconds.
Document 5: The 128th Boat Race took place on 27 March 1982.
Oxford won by three-and-a-quarter lengths, securing their seventh consecutive victory.
Document 6: The 31st Boat Race took place on the 28 March 1874.
Cambridge won by three and a half lengths in their fifth consecutive victory.
Document 7: The 52nd Boat Race took place on 30 March 1895.
Oxford won by two-and-a-quarter lengths in a time of 20 minutes 50 seconds.

topic.name: Historical Sites and Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical sites and architectural structures in England and Wales, highlighting their significance, history, and conservation.

Document 1: St Mary 's Church is an active Anglican parish church located in Monk 's Lane , Acton , a village to the west of Nantwich , Cheshire , England.
Since 1967 it has been designated a Grade I listed building.
Document 3: The Old Church of St Gwenllwyfo , Llanwenllwyfo is a medieval ruined church near Dulas , in Anglesey , north Wales.
Restored in 1610 and again in the 18th and 19th centuries, it contained an oak screen and pulpit from 1610.
Document 4: Dunster Working Watermill ( also known as Castle Mill ) is a restored 18th century watermill , situated on the River Avill.
It is a Grade II * listed building.
Document 5: Beeston Castle is a former Royal castle in Beeston , Cheshire , England.
The castle is now in ruins.
Document 6: Cambridge Castle , locally also known as Castle Mound , is located in Cambridge , Cambridgeshire , England.

Document 7: The architecture of Scotland in the prehistoric era includes all human building within the modern borders of Scotland.

topic.name: Military Aircraft Development
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the design, development, and operational history of various military aircraft from different countries and eras.

Document 1: The Tupolev Tu-85 was a Soviet prototype strategic bomber based on the Tu-4.
Only two prototypes were built before the program was canceled.

ACG aircraft have deployed to Diego Garcia during Operation Slipper.

topic.name: Scientific and Technical Concepts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various scientific and technical topics ranging from physics and mathematics to engineering and technology.

Document 1: The five euro note is the lowest value euro banknote and has been used since the introduction of the euro in 2002.
The note contains several complex security features such as watermarks, invisible ink, holograms and microprinting that document its authenticity.
Document 2: In mathematics, a matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions, arranged in rows and columns.
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations.
Document 3: A variable-frequency drive is a type of adjustable-speed drive used in electro-mechanical drive systems to control AC motor speed and torque.
VFDs are used in applications ranging from small appliances to the largest of mine mill drives and compressors.
Document 4: Quantum electrodynamics is the relativistic quantum field theory of electrodynamics.
QED mathematically describes all phenomena involving electrically charged particles interacting by means of exchange of photons.
Document 5: A subwoofer is a woofer dedicated to the reproduction of low-pitched audio frequencies known as bass.
Subwoofers are intended to augment the low frequency range of loudspeakers covering higher frequency bands.
Document 6: The Fizeau experiment was carried out by Hippolyte Fizeau in 1851 to measure the relative speeds of light in moving water.
His results seemingly supported the partial aether-drag hypothesis of Fresnel.
Document 7: The topology of an electronic circuit is the form taken by the network of interconnections of the circuit components.
Electronic network topology is related to mathematical topology.

topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of railway infrastructure and services in Norway and the UK, including tunnels, locomotives, and railway companies.

topic.name: Naval History of World War I
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels and their roles during World War I, highlighting their construction, service, and eventual fate.

Document 1: Bruix was assigned to protect troop convoys from French North Africa to France before she was transferred to the Atlantic to support Allied operations against the German colony of Kamerun in September.
She was briefly assigned to support Allied operations in the Dardanelles in early 1915 before she began patrolling the Aegean Sea and Greek territorial waters.
Document 2: Gazelle participated in the Venezuelan crisis of 1902 – 03.

Document 3: HMS Gipsy spent most of the pre-war period as part of the Mediterranean Fleet.

Document 4: Friedrich Carl was brought back to active service to act as Rear Admiral Behring's flagship in the Baltic Sea.

Document 5: Hiei patrolled off the Chinese coast on several occasions during World War I.

Document 6: Satsuma saw no combat during World War I, although she led a squadron that occupied several German colonies in the Pacific Ocean in 1914.

Document 7: Northumberland spent her career with the Channel Fleet and occasionally served as a flagship.
She was converted into a coal hulk in 1909 and sold in 1927.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Their Impact
